action.reasoning: Observation: frozen CLIP ViT-L/14 with 2-view averaged features and an MLP head improved official macro-F1 from 0.2339 to 0.2489, with val macro-F1 around 0.291. Analysis: representation quality is helping, but the plain supervised head is still likely overfitting common classes and location-specific shortcuts. We have enough time for one stronger iteration. A high-value next step is domain generalization at the feature level: use train location labels to learn a classifier that is invariant across locations by adding a small domain-adversarial head with gradient reversal on frozen CLIP features, while keeping class-balanced loss. This is cheap because only heads train on cached features. Also add logit ensembling between original and horizontally flipped views at inference if not already stored consistently. Decision: patch model/train/predict to support a feature-level DANN-style adversarial location head on cached CLIP features, train it on ViT-L features, validate locally, and submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --model_name openai/clip-vit-large-patch14 --epochs 16 --batch_size 96 --head_batch_size 2048 --lr 0.002 --weight_decay 0.0005 --dropout 0.3 --hidden_dim 1024 --label_smoothing 0.05 --use_flip --domain_adv_weight 0.15 && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_preds.json
